title: Orchestrator Output Schema
description: Structured output produced by the Orchestrator after decomposing a validated AI CEO execution plan into ordered, routable task steps with checkpoints and a rollback strategy.

execution_plan.description: Ordered array of task steps that constitute the full execution sequence. Each step is assigned to a specific skill or sub-agent and includes all inputs, dependencies, and verification criteria required to execute and validate it.

step_id.description: Short unique identifier for this step within the execution plan (e.g., 'step-1', 'gate-phase-1').

phase_id.description: The phase from the approved AI CEO plan that this step belongs to.

skill.description: The skill or sub-agent to which this step is routed (e.g., 'dev-agent', 'gate-manager', 'test-runner', 'deploy-agent').

description.description: Human-readable description of what this step accomplishes and why it is positioned here in the sequence.

inputs.description: The structured inputs to be passed to the skill when invoking this step. Shape depends on the target skill's input contract.

depends_on.description: List of step_ids that must complete successfully before this step can begin. An empty array means this step has no dependencies and may begin immediately.

is_gate_check.description: True if this step is a governance gate check (routed to gate-manager). Gate check steps block all subsequent steps in their phase until they pass.

is_approval_pause.description: True if this step is a pause point awaiting human or policy approval. Execution must not proceed past this step until an approval signal is received.

approval_type.description: If is_approval_pause is true, the category of approval required (e.g., 'human-in-the-loop', 'deployment-sign-off').

verification.description: Verification specification for this step. Defines how to confirm the step completed correctly before advancing.

method.description: The mechanism used to verify this step. 'none' is only valid for pause steps or non-mutating read-only steps.

expected_state.description: Description of the expected system state after this step completes successfully. This is the contract for verification.

failure_action.description: What to do if verification fails for this step.

checkpoint_strategy.description: The strategy governing when and how execution checkpoints are evaluated throughout the run.

checkpoint_frequency.description: How frequently checkpoints are evaluated. 'after-every-step' is the most conservative and is recommended for safe mode.

inter_phase_gate.description: Whether a gate-manager check is inserted at each phase boundary before advancing to the next phase.

verification_agent.description: The skill or agent responsible for executing verification at each checkpoint.

failure_threshold.description: The number of consecutive step failures that triggers an automatic full rollback. 0 means rollback on any single failure.

rollback_plan.description: The rollback strategy for the full execution. Defines how to safely undo work at each phase boundary if verification fails.

summary.description: High-level description of the rollback strategy and any important caveats (e.g., operations that cannot be rolled back, data that may be affected).

phase_rollbacks.description: Phase-level rollback definitions. Each entry defines what must be undone if the run fails at or after the named phase.

phase_id.description: The phase this rollback entry covers.

trigger_condition.description: The condition under which this phase rollback is triggered.

rollback_steps.description: Ordered list of rollback steps to execute if this phase must be rolled back.

skill.description: The skill or agent to invoke for this rollback step.

estimated_steps.description: The total number of steps in the execution_plan array. Used by the runtime to track progress and detect incomplete plans.
